title: pilosella gracilis
intro: Pilosella gracilis, also known as lesser false sowthistle, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to Europe and western Asia. It is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its flavonoid and sesquiterpene lactone content. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, eczema, and gastrointestinal issues, and has been reported to have antioxidant, antiviral, and antifungal activities. Pilosella gracilis can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is also used in some traditional herbal remedies, such as creams and ointments, to promote wound healing and skin health.
